Off Calendar. Susan Gibson's demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and Defendant's response to the amended complaint is now due by November 28, 2022.

Motion For Order Substituting Personal Representative For Deceased Cross-Defendant And Granting Leave To File A First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-03-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
John S. Rueppel, Esq., O'NEIL & RUEPPEL, LLP, (415) 693-0550, john@jkzllp.com, for Cross-Complainant O'NEIL & RUEPPEL, LLP
No other appearances.

Matter is not contested/argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT O'NEIL & RUEPPEL, LLP's motion to substitute personal representative and file a first amended cross-complaint is granted.

Movant establishes good cause to file the amended cross-complaint. (See CCP sec. 377.41; Probate Code sec. 9370.) Ms. Gibson's opposing arguments lack merit. She is not entitled to a new notice regarding mandatory arbitration because decedent waived those rights when he commenced this action. (See Business & Professions Code sec. 6200(b)(2); Aguilar v. Lerner (2004) 32 Cal.4th 974, 989-90 [once client files a malpractice suit, client waives mandatory arbitration rights].) Ms. Gibson's arguments regarding purported flaws in the proposed amended cross-complaint should be made by a subsequent demurrer or motion.

Order is signed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)

Motion To Tax Entire Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Jan-05-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Bernard C. Jasper, Esq., JASPER LAW, 714-321-2401, for Plaintiff, Steven E. Gibson, Deceased,
No other appearances.

Counsel for plaintiff did not properly contest the Court's tentative ruling.

No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STEVEN GIBSON'S Motion To Tax Entire Memorandum Of Costs

Steven Gibson's "motion to tax entire memorandum of costs requested by defendants" is denied. Gibson sued defendants alleging attorney malpractice; they cross-claimed. Gibson died in July 2021. Instead of moving under CCP 377.31 to continue Gibson's case through a personal representative, his lawyer dismissed Gibson's claims against defendants on November 3, 2021 without prejudice. Defendants filed a costs memorandum within the 15-day deadline set by CRC 3.1700. Again acting on the deceased Gibson's behalf, his lawyer filed this motion to tax without seeking to appoint a personal representative under CCP 377.31. However, Gibson's lawyer cites no authority for such a motion, and defendants were merely protecting their rights to costs.

Counsel for the defendant is required to submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument modifies the tentative ruling and rules as follows:

Motion For Protective Order For An Extension Of Time To Respond To Discovery

The plaintiff's motion for protective order is denied. Plaintiff has failed to meet his burden of proving that his physical and mental condition prevents him or his attorneys, or his family or his friends, to work together to obtain the responses to the discovery which are in his possession, custody or control. The verified discovery responses to all discovery except the request for production of documents are due on or before April 2, 2021. The verified discovery responses to the request for production of documents including the documents, are due on or before May 3, 2021.

Defense Counsel to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling approved as to form by email to the Judge Pro Tem Philip Andersen at philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com
